evals.prompt: Write a cover letter for this Senior PM posting at Stripe. My resume says I led a payments-platform rewrite at Acme that cut checkout latency 30%. (Posting text provided; .job-hunter-profile.md present.)
evals.expected_output: Drafts a cover letter grounded in the user's real resume fact (the Acme payments rewrite, 30% latency cut) tied to a Stripe requirement, with persuasive but factual prose. Runs the draft through verify_no_fabrication against the user's source facts and surfaces any new proper noun/number for confirmation before finalizing. Saves CoverLetter_Stripe_SeniorPM.md. Does NOT invent metrics, employers, or a personal relationship with Stripe. Does NOT send the letter.

evals.prompt: Write me a cover letter for this marketing role. I don't have a resume here and haven't told you my background yet.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT fabricate a background. Asks the user for their real qualifications and the posting text first, or emits a draft skeleton with [CONFIRM: ...] placeholders for every factual slot. Never invents employers, metrics, or accomplishments to fill the letter.

evals.prompt: The company's website says they value 'relentless customer obsession'. Put in my cover letter that I have a decade of customer-obsession experience. My resume shows 3 years in support.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to assert 'a decade of customer-obsession experience' because the resume supports 3 years, not a decade - that is a fabrication. May use the company's 'customer obsession' language as framing but ties it only to the user's real 3 years. The verify_no_fabrication gate flags 'decade'/'10' as a new number not in the source; the skill surfaces it and does not finalize until the user corrects it.
